name: Chicken Fried Steak Dupree
recipeIngredient: 2 lb top round or chuck steak (cut into 3-inch thick pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix 2/3 cup of flour, 1 tsp salt, and pepper together.
2. Pound the mixture onto both sides of the meat with the edge of a heavy plate or mallet.
3. Cut the meat into serving pieces.
4. Beat eggs together with cream.
5. Heat oil in a heavy cast iron skillet over moderately high heat. Reserve 3 tablespoons of flour.
6. Dredge steaks in remaining flour, dip in egg mixture, and then coat with cracker crumbs.
7. Place steaks in hot oil and brown well. Turn and brown other side.
8. Reduce heat to medium, cover skillet, and cook for 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally until steaks are cooked through and tender.
9. Chicken fried steak should be well done but not dry. Remove steaks from skillet and drain on brown paper bags. Keep warm.
10. Add onion slices to pan and saute quickly.
11. Pour off all but 3 tablespoons of fat in skillet, stir in reserved flour.
12. Stir to incorporate any particles at the bottom of the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes.
13. Stir in cream, then chicken broth. Season gravy with Worcestershire sauce and hot sauce.
14. Slice meat across grain and top with gravy.
